title: release.yml has no publish path for ca-codex; the plugin can never be tagged/released via the sanctioned mechanism
evidence: release.yml's only job (`release`) is hardcoded to ca: it reads `plugins/ca/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (line 43: `MANIFEST=$(node -p "require('./plugins/ca/.claude-plugin/plugin.json').version")`), tags `v$MANIFEST` (line 50), and extracts release notes from the repo-root `CHANGELOG.md` (line 59, `## [$VER]`). There is no second job, no input to select a plugin, and no ca-codex-namespaced tag scheme (contrast with ci.yml's ca-sandbox version-bump job, which is a full per-plugin twin using a `ca-sandbox-v*` tag namespace, ci.yml:291-340). ca-codex's manifest (`plugins/ca-codex/.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, version 0.1.0) is never read anywhere in release.yml.
impact: The only way to cut a GitHub Release/tag for ca-codex is by hand outside the sanctioned workflow, or a maintainer runs the ca release with confirm=<ca's version> believing it also republishes ca-codex — it does not. Marketplace consumers pulling `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` -> `./plugins/ca-codex` have no corresponding tagged release artifact tied to CI; a version bump to ca-codex's plugin.json produces no CHANGELOG-driven, verified, idempotent publish the way ca and ca-sandbox get.
recommendation: Add a ca-codex twin release job (mirroring the ca-sandbox version-bump twin pattern already in ci.yml): parameterize on `plugins/ca-codex/.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, a `ca-codex-v*` tag namespace, and either a ca-codex CHANGELOG section or an explicit decision that ca-codex ships notes-free until M2/M3 warrants a CHANGELOG.
acceptance_criteria: release.yml (or a sibling workflow) can tag and publish a GitHub Release for ca-codex from its own plugin.json version, independent of ca's release cycle
the new job is idempotent (re-run on an existing tag/release is a no-op) like the existing ca job
